task_completion.description: Navigate to the Custom Field Extraction settings in ManageEngine EventLog Analyzer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The ManageEngine EventLog Analyzer web interface is open, showing the 'Custom Field Extraction' or 'Field Extraction' configuration page.

task_completion.description: Target the 'FinCore' application logs for the extraction rule.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The log source, application name, or filter condition in the extraction setup is set to 'FinCore'.

task_completion.description: Enter the new custom field name.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'FinTransactionID' is typed into the field name input box.

task_completion.description: Enter a regex pattern to capture the transaction IDs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A regular expression containing 'TXN' (e.g., TXN-\d{6} or similar) is visible in the regex pattern input box.

task_completion.description: Save the custom field extraction configuration.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success message indicating the rule was saved, or the 'FinTransactionID' rule is visible in the list/table of configured custom field extractions.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the ManageEngine EventLog Analyzer web interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: A web browser is open showing the actual ManageEngine EventLog Analyzer application, not a terminal or script editor.

integrity.description: The agent did not mock the answer in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The inputs ('FinTransactionID', 'FinCore', regex) are entered into the actual application's form fields, not written in Notepad, gedit, or similar text editors.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely saved the configuration in the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final state shows the application's native saved state or success toast, rather than just a filled-out form that was never submitted.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target application log name is 'FinCore'. The custom field name to be created must be exactly 'FinTransactionID'. The regex pattern must be designed to capture the format 'TXN-######', so look for the fragment 'TXN' in the regex input.
